summary: Tested two P1 features end-to-end: (1) Recurring Journal Templates (CRUD + use_count + soft-delete + trash restore) in Books → Activity → Manual Journal modal, and (2) Bulk CSV vendor-bill import (preview + commit + GL posting + csv_import flag) in Payables. Backend: 9/9 pytest passed — 6/6 existing tests in test_journal_templates_and_csv.py plus 3 new spec-coverage tests in test_journal_templates_csv_extras.py (trash+restore, header synonyms 'vendor_name'/'invoice_date' + due_date defaulting to bill_date, csv_import flag verified directly in MongoDB). Frontend: Manual Journal modal opens, templates-toolbar visible, save-btn correctly disabled when unbalanced/empty and enabled after balanced lines, save → dropdown updates → load → delete-btn appears → delete removes from dropdown. Payables: Bulk CSV button visible next to other actions, modal opens, step1 (entity dropdown + file picker + Parse&Preview) works, step2 (valid/invalid badges, csv-row-{n} rows with vendor highlighted red when unmatched, GL DR/CR preview, errors list, Commit N Bills), step3 (success header '2 bills created', table of created bills, Done) works and refreshed list shows the imported bills (UI-CSV-A visible after close). Apostrophe in help text 'vendor\'s category default' renders correctly (no raw ' leaking). All acceptance criteria met.

issues: React 19 hydration warning in console: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Likely a styled <span> inside an <option> in the load-template <select>. Non-blocking (dropdown still works) but should be cleaned up to keep console error-free.

action_items: (Minor / Non-blocking) Remove the <span> child inside <option> in the template-load-select to silence React 19 hydration warning. Plain text on options is the canonical fix.
critical_code_review_comments: Journal-template endpoints correctly snapshot account_number/account_name/account_type onto each line at create time — verified via pytest assertions tpl.lines[0].account_number == '3900' (so renaming the underlying GL account later doesn't corrupt the template).
Duplicate-name validation on POST and PUT both correctly return 400 against the same entity_id (test_template_crud_lifecycle covers both).
POST /api/books/journal-templates/{id}/use increments use_count and updates last_used_at — verified via PUT response use_count>=1 after use call.
Soft-delete sets is_deleted=true and the template appears in /api/trash/journal_templates AND is restorable via POST /api/trash/journal_templates/{id}/restore — verified end-to-end.
CSV preview header validation correctly returns {ok:false, header_error:'...missing required columns...'} (no 4xx — explicit envelope as spec requires).
CSV header synonyms work: 'vendor_name' resolves to vendor input, 'invoice_date' resolves to bill_date, and an empty due_date column defaults to bill_date (spec compliance verified).
CSV amount parsing handles: '1500.00', '$2,400.50' (quoted), '(100.00)' → -100.0 (parens negative), and 'not-a-number' produces row.valid=false with 'amount' error message.
CSV preview date parsing accepts both ISO ('2026-02-01') and US MM/DD/YYYY ('02/02/2026') formats.
CSV commit creates bills via the normal GL pipeline (DR expense / CR 2000 AP) — verified by GET /api/vendor-bills/{id} returning status='Pending' and matching total/vendor_id.

CSV commit persists csv_import=true and notes='Imported from CSV bulk upload' to MongoDB (verified directly via motor query — note: csv_import is stripped from the API response by the Pydantic model, but exists in DB as spec requires).
CSV commit correctly skips rows with missing vendor_id or expense_account_id and returns them in 'skipped' with a 'reason' field; created_count and skipped_count are accurate.
Frontend Save-as-Template button correctly disabled when no balanced lines (verified) and enabled after balanced (verified) — guarding against saving empty/unbalanced templates.
Frontend csv-row-{rowNumber} testid pattern populates correctly, with red-styled vendor name for unmatched vendors and a 'Valid' badge for matched rows.
After commit, the bills list refreshes and the newly imported bills (UI-CSV-A in test) appear immediately in the All Bills table.
seed_data_creation: All test data created and cleaned up: (1) journal templates prefixed TEST_TPL_/TEST_TRASH_TPL_/TEST_UI_TPL_ (soft-deleted via API at end), (2) vendors prefixed CSV_TEST_VENDOR_/CSV_UI_VENDOR_ (deleted via API), (3) vendor bills with bill_number prefixes CSV-A/CSV-B/FLAG-1/UI-CSV-A/UI-CSV-B (deleted via API in test teardowns).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 backend tests can be re-run with: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_journal_templates_and_csv.py tests/test_journal_templates_csv_extras.py -v. The csv_import flag is only persisted in MongoDB — the GET /api/vendor-bills/{id} Pydantic response model does not include it (verified intentional). For the Bulk CSV UI flow, important detail: CSV values with embedded commas like '$2,400.50' must be quoted in the test CSV otherwise the CSV parser will split them (the backend correctly handles both — quoted in pytest, unquoted in my UI test which yielded $2.00, not a bug). One minor console warning: React 19 hydration error about <span> child of <option> in template-load-select.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — both P1 features implemented correctly per spec. One non-blocking console warning noted as an action item.
